Programming Languages.name: Java
Programming Languages.description: General-purpose object-oriented programming language designed to run on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

Programming Languages.name: C
Programming Languages.notes: Foundational systems programming language that influenced many modern languages including C++, C#, Java, and Go. Common alternatives: C++, Rust.
Programming Languages.description: General-purpose compiled programming language widely used for operating systems, embedded systems, drivers, and performance-critical software.

Programming Languages.name: C++
Programming Languages.notes: General-purpose systems programming language extending C with object-oriented and generic programming features. Common alternatives: C, Rust.
Programming Languages.description: General-purpose compiled programming language widely used for systems software, game engines, embedded systems, desktop applications, and performance-critical applications.

Programming Languages.name: Rust
Programming Languages.description: A systems programming language focused on performance and memory safety.

Markup & Data Languages.name: CSS
Markup & Data Languages.notes: Primary styling language for web pages. Common alternatives: SASS, LESS.
Markup & Data Languages.description: Language used to define the presentation, layout, and visual appearance of HTML documents and web applications.

Frameworks & Libraries.name: Angular
Frameworks & Libraries.notes: Modern Angular framework; distinct from AngularJS. Common alternatives: React, Vue.js, Svelte.
Frameworks & Libraries.description: TypeScript-based web application framework maintained by Google for building single-page and enterprise web applications.

Platforms & Runtimes.name: .NET
Platforms & Runtimes.notes: Modern cross-platform successor to the original .NET Framework. Common alternatives: JVM, Node.js.
Platforms & Runtimes.description: Microsoft's software development platform and runtime for building web, desktop, cloud, mobile, and enterprise applications.

IDEs & Editors.name: Android Studio
IDEs & Editors.notes: Official IDE for Android development. Common alternatives: IntelliJ IDEA, Visual Studio Code, Xcode (for iOS development).
IDEs & Editors.description: Official IDE for Android application development based on IntelliJ IDEA and maintained by Google.

Version Control.name: Git
Version Control.notes: Distributed version control system. Common alternatives: SVN, Mercurial.
Version Control.description: Distributed version control system used to track changes in source code and coordinate development across teams.

Build & Dependency Tools.name: Jenkins
Build & Dependency Tools.notes: Open-source automation server commonly used for CI/CD pipelines. Common alternatives: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, CircleCI.
Build & Dependency Tools.description: Open-source automation server used to build, test, and deploy software through automated pipelines.

Operating Systems.name: Linux
Operating Systems.notes: Open-source Unix-like operating system family. Common alternatives: Windows, MacOS, UNIX.
Operating Systems.description: Open-source Unix-like operating system kernel and ecosystem widely used on servers, cloud infrastructure, embedded devices, and developer workstations.

Database Systems.name: MongoDB
Database Systems.notes: Document-oriented NoSQL database. Common alternatives: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Cloud Firestore.
Database Systems.description: Document-oriented NoSQL database that stores data in flexible JSON-like documents rather than relational tables.

Database Models.name: RDBMS
Database Models.notes: Most common database model used in enterprise applications. Common alternatives: NoSQL.
Database Models.description: Database management system based on the relational model, storing data in tables composed of rows and columns and accessed primarily through SQL.

Cloud Platforms.name: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
Cloud Platforms.notes: Leading cloud computing platform. Common alternatives: Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform.
Cloud Platforms.description: Cloud computing platform provided by Amazon offering infrastructure, platform, database, networking, storage, AI, and application services.

Cloud Service Models.name: IaaS
Cloud Service Models.notes: Cloud service model providing virtualized infrastructure resources. Common alternatives: PaaS, SaaS.
Cloud Service Models.description: Cloud computing model where infrastructure resources such as virtual machines, networking, and storage are provided as managed services.

Containerization & Orchestration.name: Docker
Containerization & Orchestration.notes: Popular containerization platform used to package and deploy applications consistently across environments. Common alternatives: Podman, containerd.
Containerization & Orchestration.description: Platform for building, packaging, distributing, and running applications inside lightweight containers.

Networking.name: Mininet
Networking.notes: Network emulation platform commonly used in networking education, research, and Software-Defined Networking (SDN). Common alternatives: NetSim, GNS3, EVE-NG.
Networking.description: Open-source network emulation platform that creates realistic virtual networks using lightweight hosts, switches, links, and controllers on a single machine.

Security.name: System Penetration Testing
Security.notes: Authorized security assessment used to identify vulnerabilities before attackers do. Common alternatives: Vulnerability Scanning, Security Auditing.
Security.description: Authorized simulation of cyberattacks against systems, applications, or networks to evaluate security controls and identify vulnerabilities.

Web Servers.name: Apache
Web Servers.notes: One of the most widely used web servers. Common alternatives: nginx, IIS.
Web Servers.description: Open-source web server software used to host websites, web applications, and APIs by serving HTTP requests.
